disrupt.en: interrupt something (a process, event, or activity) by causing a disturbance

ex.en: The heavy snowfall disrupted train services across the country.
ex.ko: 폭설로 전국의 기차 운행에 지장이 생겼다.

dissatisfaction.en: a feeling of being unhappy because something did not meet your expectations

ex.en: Customers expressed dissatisfaction with the slow delivery service.
ex.ko: 고객들은 느린 배송 서비스에 불만을 표했다.

dissatisfied.en: not happy or pleased with something

ex.en: Many employees are dissatisfied with their current salary.
ex.ko: 많은 직원들이 현재 급여에 불만족스러워한다.

dissident.en: a person who publicly disagrees with and criticizes their government

ex.en: Several dissidents were arrested for criticizing the regime.
ex.ko: 몇몇 반체제 인사들이 정권을 비판했다는 이유로 체포되었다.

dissident.en: disagreeing with, or opposing, an official or established belief or system

ex.en: The magazine published a dissident opinion on economic policy.
ex.ko: 그 잡지는 경제 정책에 대한 반체제적 견해를 실었다.

distinguished.en: successful, respected, and admired by other people, often because of an impressive appearance or achievement

ex.en: She had a long and distinguished career as a diplomat.
ex.ko: 그녀는 외교관으로서 길고 저명한 경력을 쌓았다.

distortion.en: a false or misleading representation of facts or information

ex.en: The report was criticized as a distortion of the truth.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 진실의 왜곡이라는 비판을 받았다.

distortion.en: a change in the natural shape, sound, or appearance of something

ex.en: There was some distortion in the audio recording.
ex.ko: 그 녹음 파일에는 약간의 음 왜곡이 있었다.

distract.en: take someone's attention away from what they are doing or thinking about

ex.en: Loud music outside kept distracting him from his homework.
ex.ko: 밖에서 나는 시끄러운 음악 소리가 계속 그의 숙제를 방해했다.

distraction.en: something that takes your attention away from what you should be doing

ex.en: Turn off your phone to avoid distractions while studying.
ex.ko: 공부할 때 방해 요소를 없애려면 휴대폰을 꺼 둬라.

distraction.en: an activity that provides entertainment and takes your mind off your worries

ex.en: Watching movies is a good distraction from stress.
ex.ko: 영화 감상은 스트레스에서 벗어나는 좋은 기분전환거리다.

distressing.en: causing feelings of worry, sadness, or pain

ex.en: It was distressing to hear about the accident.
ex.ko: 그 사고 소식을 듣는 것은 괴로운 일이었다.

distributor.en: a company or person that supplies goods to shops or other businesses

ex.en: The company hired a new distributor to sell its products overseas.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 해외에 제품을 판매할 새 유통업자를 고용했다.

diversion.en: a route that traffic must use when the usual road is closed

ex.en: There's a diversion ahead because of roadworks.
ex.ko: 도로 공사 때문에 앞쪽에 우회로가 있다.

diversion.en: an activity that provides pleasant amusement or relaxation

ex.en: Reading offers a welcome diversion from daily stress.
ex.ko: 독서는 일상의 스트레스에서 벗어나는 반가운 기분전환거리가 된다.

divert.en: make someone or something change direction or route

ex.en: Police diverted traffic away from the accident scene.
ex.ko: 경찰은 사고 현장에서 벗어나도록 차량을 우회시켰다.

divert.en: take someone's attention away from something

ex.en: The government tried to divert public attention from the scandal.
ex.ko: 정부는 스캔들에서 대중의 관심을 다른 곳으로 돌리려 했다.

divided.en: having very different opinions so that people cannot agree

ex.en: The committee was divided over the new proposal.
ex.ko: 위원회는 새 제안을 두고 의견이 갈렸다.

division.en: a section or department of a large organization

ex.en: She works in the sales division of the company.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 회사의 영업 부서에서 일한다.

division.en: the arithmetic operation of dividing one number by another

ex.en: The children are learning multiplication and division at school.
ex.ko: 아이들은 학교에서 곱셈과 나눗셈을 배우고 있다.

division.en: disagreement between people that separates them into different groups

ex.en: The vote revealed deep divisions within the party.
ex.ko: 그 투표는 당 내부의 깊은 분열을 드러냈다.

division.en: a large unit of an army

ex.en: The general commanded an entire division.
ex.ko: 그 장군은 사단 전체를 지휘했다.

divorce.en: the legal ending of a marriage

ex.en: They went through a difficult divorce last year.
ex.ko: 그들은 작년에 힘든 이혼 과정을 겪었다.

ex.en: They decided to divorce after years of conflict.
ex.ko: 그들은 수년간의 갈등 끝에 이혼하기로 했다.

divorce.en: separate two things that are usually connected

ex.en: It's hard to divorce the artist's work from his personal life.
ex.ko: 그 예술가의 작품을 그의 사생활과 떼어놓고 보기는 어렵다.

dna.en: the substance in the cells of living things that carries genetic information

ex.en: Scientists analyzed the DNA found at the crime scene.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 범죄 현장에서 발견된 DNA를 분석했다.

dna.en: the most basic and characteristic quality of someone or something

ex.en: Innovation is part of the company's DNA.
ex.ko: 혁신은 그 회사의 근본적인 특성 중 하나다.

dock.en: a place where ships are loaded, unloaded, or repaired

ex.en: The ship was tied up at the dock overnight.
ex.ko: 그 배는 밤새 부두에 정박해 있었다.

dock.en: (of a ship) come into a harbor and stop next to a dock

ex.en: The cruise ship docked in the harbor early in the morning.
ex.ko: 그 크루즈선은 이른 아침 항구에 정박했다.

dock.en: take away part of someone's money or points as a punishment

ex.en: He was docked a day's pay for being late.
ex.ko: 그는 지각으로 하루치 임금이 삭감되었다.

domestic.en: relating to a particular country rather than to other countries

ex.en: The airline offers both domestic and international flights.
ex.ko: 그 항공사는 국내선과 국제선을 모두 운항한다.

domestic.en: relating to the home or family life

ex.en: She shares domestic chores with her husband.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남편과 집안일을 분담한다.

domestic.en: (of an animal) kept as a pet or on a farm rather than wild

ex.en: Cats are one of the most common domestic animals.
ex.ko: 고양이는 가장 흔한 가축 중 하나다.

dominate.en: have controlling power or influence over someone or something

ex.en: The company dominates the smartphone market.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 스마트폰 시장을 지배하고 있다.

donate.en: give money or goods to a charity or good cause

ex.en: He donated a large sum of money to the hospital.
ex.ko: 그는 병원에 거액을 기부했다.

donation.en: money or goods given to a charity or good cause

ex.en: The charity relies on public donations.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 대중의 기부에 의존한다.

donkey.cue: 말과 비슷하지만 작고 귀가 긴 동물
donkey.en: an animal like a small horse with long ears

ex.en: The farmer used a donkey to carry heavy loads.
ex.ko: 그 농부는 무거운 짐을 나르는 데 당나귀를 이용했다.

doom.en: a terrible fate, especially death or destruction, that cannot be avoided

ex.en: The old ship was headed for doom in the storm.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 배는 폭풍 속에서 파멸을 향해 가고 있었다.

doom.en: make something certain to fail or be destroyed

ex.en: The project was doomed from the start due to poor planning.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 부실한 계획 때문에 처음부터 실패할 운명이었다.

doorkeeper.en: a person whose job is to guard or watch the entrance of a building

ex.en: The doorkeeper checked everyone's ID before letting them in.
ex.ko: 그 문지기는 사람들을 들여보내기 전에 신분증을 확인했다.

doorway.en: the space in a wall where a door is, used for entering or leaving a room

ex.en: She stood in the doorway, waiting for a reply.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문간에 서서 대답을 기다렸다.

doorway.en: a way that leads to a new opportunity or stage in life

ex.en: Getting this internship could be a doorway to a great career.
ex.ko: 이 인턴십을 얻는 것이 훌륭한 커리어로 가는 관문이 될 수도 있다.

dorm.en: (informal) a building where students live at a college or university

ex.en: She lives in a dorm on campus.
ex.ko: 그녀는 캠퍼스 안 기숙사에서 산다.

dormitory.en: a building at a school or college where students live

ex.en: The university built a new dormitory for freshmen.
ex.ko: 그 대학은 신입생을 위한 새 기숙사를 지었다.

dormitory.cue: 침대 여러 개가 있는 큰 방
dormitory.en: a large room with several beds, often in a school or hostel

ex.en: The hostel offers cheap beds in a shared dormitory.
ex.ko: 그 호스텔은 공동 침실에 저렴한 침대를 제공한다.

dose.en: an amount of a medicine that is taken at one time

ex.en: Take one dose of this medicine every eight hours.
ex.ko: 이 약은 8시간마다 한 번씩 복용하세요.

dose.en: give someone a measured amount of medicine

ex.en: The nurse dosed the patient with painkillers.
ex.ko: 간호사는 환자에게 진통제를 투여했다.

doubly.en: to twice the usual degree or amount

ex.en: Please be doubly careful when crossing this road.
ex.ko: 이 길을 건널 때는 이중으로 조심하세요.

doubtful.en: not likely to be true or to happen

ex.en: It's doubtful that the flight will leave on time.
ex.ko: 그 비행기가 제시간에 출발할지는 의심스럽다.

doubtful.en: feeling unsure or not convinced about something

ex.en: I'm doubtful about his plan to quit his job.
ex.ko: 나는 그가 직장을 그만두려는 계획에 대해 확신이 서지 않는다.

dove.en: a bird, often white, that is a symbol of peace

ex.en: A white dove flew over the peace rally.
ex.ko: 흰 비둘기 한 마리가 평화 집회 위로 날아갔다.

dove.en: a person who prefers peaceful negotiation over war or conflict, especially in politics

ex.en: The senator is known as a dove on foreign policy.
ex.ko: 그 상원의원은 외교 정책에서 온건파로 알려져 있다.

downfall.en: a sudden loss of power, position, or success

ex.en: Greed was the downfall of the once-powerful company.
ex.ko: 한때 막강했던 그 회사의 몰락 원인은 탐욕이었다.

downsize.en: reduce the number of employees or the size of a company or organization

ex.en: The firm decided to downsize its workforce by 10 percent.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 인력을 10퍼센트 감원하기로 결정했다.

dowry.cue: 결혼할 때 신부 측이 가져오는 돈·재산
dowry.en: money or property that a woman's family gives to her husband when she gets married

ex.en: In some cultures, the bride's family still provides a dowry.
ex.ko: 일부 문화권에서는 여전히 신부 측 가족이 지참금을 준비한다.

draft.en: an early or rough version of a piece of writing

ex.en: She sent me the first draft of her essay.
ex.ko: 그녀는 에세이 초고를 나에게 보냈다.

draft.en: write a rough version of a piece of writing

ex.en: He drafted a letter of complaint to the company.
ex.ko: 그는 그 회사에 보낼 항의 편지 초안을 작성했다.

draft.en: the system of ordering people to join the armed forces

ex.en: He was called up in the draft during the war.
ex.ko: 그는 전쟁 중 징집되었다.

draft.en: a current of cold air blowing through a room

ex.en: There's a draft coming from under the door.
ex.ko: 문 밑으로 외풍이 들어온다.

draft.en: beer served from a barrel rather than from a bottle or can

ex.en: Two drafts, please.
ex.ko: 생맥주 두 잔 주세요.

drain.en: a pipe or hole that carries away waste water

ex.en: The kitchen drain is blocked again.
ex.ko: 부엌 배수구가 또 막혔다.

ex.en: Drain the pasta before adding the sauce.
ex.ko: 소스를 넣기 전에 파스타의 물을 빼세요.

drain.en: gradually use up someone's money, energy, or resources

ex.en: The long commute drains all my energy.
ex.ko: 긴 통근 시간이 내 기력을 모두 소모시킨다.

drain.en: a gradual loss of something valuable, such as money, energy, or talented people

ex.en: The country is suffering from a brain drain of young professionals.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 젊은 전문 인력의 유출로 어려움을 겪고 있다.

dramatically.en: in a way that is sudden and very noticeable

ex.en: Sales increased dramatically after the ad campaign.
ex.ko: 광고 캠페인 이후 매출이 극적으로 증가했다.

drapery.en: heavy curtains, often hung in folds

ex.en: Thick drapery covered the tall windows.
ex.ko: 두꺼운 커튼이 높은 창문을 덮고 있었다.

dread.en: feel great fear or worry about something that is going to happen

ex.en: She dreads going to the dentist.
ex.ko: 그녀는 치과에 가는 것을 몹시 두려워한다.

dread.en: a strong feeling of fear about something that may happen

ex.en: He felt a sense of dread before the interview.
ex.ko: 그는 면접 전에 두려움을 느꼈다.

ex.en: We had dreadful weather during our trip.
ex.ko: 우리는 여행 중에 지독한 날씨를 겪었다.

dreamy.en: seeming to be thinking about something else, not paying attention to what is around you

ex.en: She had a dreamy look in her eyes during the lecture.
ex.ko: 그녀는 강의 시간 내내 눈빛이 꿈꾸는 듯 멍했다.

dreamy.en: beautiful and peaceful in a way that seems almost unreal

ex.en: They spent a dreamy weekend by the lake.
ex.ko: 그들은 호숫가에서 몽환적인 주말을 보냈다.

ex.en: Is the tap water here drinkable?
ex.ko: 여기 수돗물은 마실 수 있나요?

droop.en: a downward, sagging position caused by tiredness or weakness

ex.en: There was a noticeable droop in his shoulders after the long shift.
ex.ko: 긴 근무 후 그의 어깨는 눈에 띄게 축 처져 있었다.

droop.en: bend or hang down weakly, especially from tiredness or lack of water

ex.en: The flowers began to droop without water.
ex.ko: 꽃들이 물 없이 시들기 시작했다.

dropout.cue: 학교를 끝까지 마치지 않고 그만둔 사람
dropout.en: someone who leaves school before finishing their course

ex.en: He was a college dropout who later became a successful entrepreneur.
ex.ko: 그는 대학 중퇴자였지만 나중에 성공한 사업가가 되었다.

drought.en: a long period of time with little or no rain

ex.en: The region suffered a severe drought last summer.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 지난여름 심한 가뭄을 겪었다.

drought.en: a long period during which there is a lack of something

ex.en: The team is going through a goal drought this season.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 이번 시즌 득점 가뭄을 겪고 있다.

duel.en: a fight arranged in advance between two people using weapons, usually over a matter of honor

ex.en: The two men fought a duel at dawn.
ex.ko: 두 남자는 새벽에 결투를 벌였다.

duel.en: an intense contest between two skillful opponents

ex.en: The match turned into a duel between the two star players.
ex.ko: 그 경기는 두 스타 선수 간의 치열한 대결이 되었다.

ex.en: That was a really dumb mistake to make.
ex.ko: 그건 정말 멍청한 실수였다.

dumb.en: temporarily unable to speak, often from shock or surprise

ex.en: He was struck dumb with fear.
ex.ko: 그는 두려움에 말문이 막혔다.

ex.en: Keep a duplicate of your passport in a safe place.
ex.ko: 여권 사본은 안전한 곳에 보관해라.

duplicate.en: exactly the same as something else

ex.en: She was issued a duplicate key for her office.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사무실 여벌 열쇠를 발급받았다.

duplicate.en: make an exact copy of something

ex.en: Please duplicate this document for the meeting.
ex.ko: 회의를 위해 이 문서를 복사해 주세요.

duplication.en: the act of making an exact copy of something

ex.en: The duplication of the files took only a few minutes.
ex.ko: 그 파일들을 복제하는 데는 몇 분밖에 걸리지 않았다.

duplication.cue: 같은 일이 불필요하게 두 번 일어나서
duplication.en: a situation in which the same thing is done or exists more than once unnecessarily

ex.en: The merger will help avoid duplication of effort between the two departments.
ex.ko: 그 합병은 두 부서 간 업무 중복을 피하는 데 도움이 될 것이다.

dwarf.cue: 크기 차이 때문에 상대적으로 작아 보이게
dwarf.en: make something seem small by comparison

ex.en: The new skyscraper dwarfs all the buildings around it.
ex.ko: 그 새 마천루는 주변 건물들을 모두 왜소해 보이게 만든다.

dwarf.en: (in stories) a small legendary creature shaped like a little old man

ex.en: The story features seven dwarfs living in the forest.
ex.ko: 그 이야기에는 숲에 사는 일곱 난쟁이가 나온다.

dwarf.en: a plant, animal, or star that is much smaller than is typical for its kind

ex.en: Pluto is now classified as a dwarf planet.
ex.ko: 명왕성은 이제 왜소행성으로 분류된다.

dye.en: a substance used to change the color of something, such as fabric or hair

ex.en: She used a natural dye to color the fabric.
ex.ko: 그녀는 천을 물들이기 위해 천연 염료를 사용했다.

dye.en: change the color of something using a special substance

ex.en: She dyed her hair bright red.
ex.ko: 그녀는 머리를 빨갛게 염색했다.

dynamic.en: full of energy and new ideas; always active or changing

ex.en: She is a dynamic leader who inspires her team.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팀에 영감을 주는 역동적인 리더다.

dynamic.en: the force or way that people or things interact and change within a group or situation

ex.en: The power dynamic between the two countries shifted after the war.
ex.ko: 전쟁 이후 두 나라 간의 역학 관계가 바뀌었다.

eagerly.en: in a way that shows strong interest or excitement about something

ex.en: The children eagerly waited for the show to start.
ex.ko: 아이들은 공연이 시작되기를 간절히 기다렸다.

eagle.en: a large bird with excellent eyesight that hunts other animals

ex.en: An eagle soared high above the mountains.
ex.ko: 독수리 한 마리가 산 위로 높이 날아올랐다.

earnings.en: the money that a person earns from working

ex.en: Her earnings have increased since she got the promotion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 승진한 이후 수입이 늘었다.

earnings.en: the profit that a company makes

ex.en: The company reported record earnings this quarter.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이번 분기에 사상 최고 수익을 기록했다고 발표했다.

ease.cue: 힘들지 않고 수월하게 할 수 있음
ease.en: the state of being simple or not difficult to do

ex.en: She solved the puzzle with ease.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 퍼즐을 쉽게 풀었다.

ease.en: a state of comfort, free from worry or pain

ex.en: He felt a sense of ease as soon as he got home.
ex.ko: 그는 집에 도착하자마자 편안함을 느꼈다.

ease.en: make pain, worry, or a problem less severe

ex.en: The medicine helped ease her headache.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 두통을 완화하는 데 도움이 되었다.

ex.en: The ship sailed eastward across the ocean.
ex.ko: 그 배는 대양을 가로질러 동쪽으로 항해했다.

eastward.en: moving or facing toward the east

ex.en: They took the eastward route to avoid the storm.
ex.ko: 그들은 폭풍을 피하려고 동쪽 방향 경로를 택했다.

ecologically.en: in a way that relates to the environment and living things

ex.en: The area is ecologically important for many rare species.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 많은 희귀종에게 생태학적으로 중요하다.

ecologist.en: a scientist who studies the relationship between living things and their environment

ex.en: Ecologists warned about the loss of the wetland habitat.
ex.ko: 생태학자들은 습지 서식지의 소실에 대해 경고했다.

economical.en: not costing or using much money, fuel, or time

ex.en: This car is very economical to run.
ex.ko: 이 차는 운행하는 데 매우 경제적이다.

economical.en: careful about spending money or using resources

ex.en: She has always been economical with her spending.
ex.ko: 그녀는 항상 지출에 있어 절약하는 편이었다.

economist.en: a person who studies or is an expert in economics

ex.en: The economist predicted a slowdown in growth next year.
ex.ko: 그 경제학자는 내년 성장 둔화를 예측했다.

ecstatic.en: feeling or showing extreme happiness and excitement

ex.en: She was ecstatic when she heard she got the job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 취업 소식을 듣고 너무 기뻐 어쩔 줄 몰랐다.

edit.en: prepare a piece of writing by correcting mistakes and making improvements

ex.en: She edited the article before it was published.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 기사가 게재되기 전에 편집했다.

edit.en: cut and arrange the different parts of a film or recording

ex.en: He spent hours editing the wedding video.
ex.ko: 그는 결혼식 영상을 편집하는 데 몇 시간을 보냈다.

edit.en: a change made to a piece of writing or media

ex.en: I made a few small edits to your report.
ex.ko: 네 보고서에 몇 가지 작은 수정을 했어.

editorial.en: an article in a newspaper that gives the opinion of the editor or publisher

ex.en: The newspaper's editorial criticized the new tax policy.
ex.ko: 그 신문의 사설은 새 세금 정책을 비판했다.

editorial.en: relating to the person or team who edits a publication

ex.en: The magazine's editorial team reviewed every submission.
ex.ko: 그 잡지의 편집팀은 모든 투고작을 검토했다.

educated.en: having received a good education; knowledgeable

ex.en: She comes from a well-educated family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교육을 잘 받은 집안 출신이다.

educated.en: based on knowledge or experience rather than just a guess

ex.en: Based on the data, I can make an educated guess about the outcome.
ex.ko: 그 데이터를 바탕으로 결과에 대해 합리적인 추측을 할 수 있다.

educator.en: a person who works in education, especially a teacher

ex.en: She has been an educator for over twenty years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 20년 넘게 교육자로 일해 왔다.

effectively.en: in a way that produces the result that was wanted

ex.en: She communicated her ideas effectively during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 자신의 생각을 효과적으로 전달했다.

effectively.en: in reality, even if not officially or exactly stated

ex.en: By missing the deadline, he effectively lost the contract.
ex.ko: 마감을 놓침으로써 그는 사실상 계약을 잃은 것이나 다름없었다.

effectiveness.cue: 어떤 방법·정책이 원하는 결과를 내는 정도
effectiveness.en: the degree to which something succeeds in producing the result that is wanted

ex.en: Researchers tested the effectiveness of the new vaccine.
ex.ko: 연구자들은 새 백신의 효과성을 시험했다.

elect.en: choose someone for a position by voting

ex.en: The citizens elected a new mayor last month.
ex.ko: 시민들은 지난달 새 시장을 선출했다.

elect.cue: 여러 방법 중 어떤 것을 하기로

ex.en: She elected to study abroad instead of staying home.
ex.ko: 그녀는 집에 머무는 대신 해외 유학을 하기로 결정했다.

elect.en: having been elected to a position but not yet holding it officially

ex.en: The president-elect will take office in January.
ex.ko: 당선인은 1월에 취임할 예정이다.

ex.en: Physical education became elective in the final year.
ex.ko: 체육은 마지막 학년에 선택 과목이 되었다.

elective.en: a course that a student can choose to take, rather than one that is required

ex.en: She took photography as an elective this semester.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이번 학기에 사진학을 선택 과목으로 들었다.

electrician.en: a person whose job is to install or repair electrical equipment

ex.en: We called an electrician to fix the wiring.
ex.ko: 우리는 배선을 고치려고 전기 기술자를 불렀다.

electronically.en: using electronic devices or the internet, rather than on paper or in person

ex.en: You can submit the form electronically.
ex.ko: 그 양식은 전자적으로 제출할 수 있다.

electronics.en: the branch of science and technology that deals with electronic devices and circuits

ex.en: He studied electronics at university.
ex.ko: 그는 대학에서 전자공학을 공부했다.

electronics.en: electronic devices, such as computers, phones, or televisions

ex.en: The store sells a wide range of consumer electronics.
ex.ko: 그 매장은 다양한 전자제품을 판매한다.

elegance.en: the quality of being graceful and stylish

ex.en: She moved across the room with elegance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우아하게 방을 가로질러 걸어갔다.

elegance.en: the quality of being pleasingly simple and clever in a solution or idea

ex.en: Mathematicians admired the elegance of the proof.
ex.ko: 수학자들은 그 증명의 간결함에 감탄했다.

elegant.en: graceful and stylish in appearance or manner

ex.en: She wore an elegant black dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 우아한 검은 드레스를 입고 갔다.

elegant.en: (of a solution or method) pleasingly simple and clever

ex.en: The engineer came up with an elegant solution to the problem.
ex.ko: 그 엔지니어는 문제에 대한 간결하고 뛰어난 해결책을 내놓았다.

elegantly.en: in a graceful and stylish way

ex.en: She was elegantly dressed for the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 행사에 우아하게 차려입었다.

elemental.en: forming a basic or essential part of something

ex.en: Trust is an elemental part of any relationship.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 모든 관계의 근본적인 요소다.

elemental.en: relating to the powerful forces of nature, such as storms or wind

ex.en: The storm had an elemental fury that terrified the sailors.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍은 선원들을 겁먹게 할 만큼 자연의 원초적인 위력을 지니고 있었다.

eligible.en: having the right qualifications or meeting the conditions needed for something

ex.en: Only full-time employees are eligible for the bonus.
ex.ko: 정규직 직원만 보너스를 받을 자격이 있다.

elite.cue: 사회에서 가장 부유하고 영향력 있는 집단
elite.en: a group of people who have the most power, wealth, or talent in a society

ex.en: Only the political elite were invited to the ceremony.
ex.ko: 정치 엘리트만이 그 행사에 초대되었다.

elite.en: belonging to the best or most skilled group of people

ex.en: She trains with an elite group of athletes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 엘리트 운동선수 그룹과 함께 훈련한다.

eloquence.cue: 말을 유창하고 설득력 있게 잘하는 능력
eloquence.en: the ability to speak or write in a way that is fluent and persuasive

ex.en: He was known for his eloquence in political debates.
ex.ko: 그는 정치 토론에서 유창한 웅변술로 유명했다.

eloquent.en: able to express ideas clearly and persuasively in speech or writing

ex.en: She gave an eloquent speech at the graduation ceremony.
ex.ko: 그녀는 졸업식에서 유창하고 설득력 있는 연설을 했다.

embassy.cue: 한 나라가 다른 나라에 두는 외교 대표부
embassy.en: the official building where a country's representatives work in a foreign country

ex.en: She applied for a visa at the embassy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대사관에서 비자를 신청했다.

embrace.en: the act of holding someone closely in your arms

ex.en: They greeted each other with a warm embrace.
ex.ko: 그들은 따뜻한 포옹으로 서로를 맞이했다.

embrace.en: hold someone closely in your arms, usually as a sign of affection

ex.en: She embraced her mother at the airport.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공항에서 어머니를 껴안았다.

embrace.en: accept something new, such as an idea or change, willingly and enthusiastically

ex.en: The company embraced new technology early on.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 일찌감치 신기술을 받아들였다.

emergence.en: the process of a new fact, idea, or thing becoming known or starting to exist

ex.en: The emergence of social media changed how people communicate.
ex.ko: 소셜 미디어의 등장은 사람들이 소통하는 방식을 바꾸었다.

emphatically.en: in a forceful and definite way

ex.en: She emphatically denied the accusation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 혐의를 단호하게 부인했다.

employ.en: pay someone to work for you

ex.en: The company employs over five hundred people.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 500명이 넘는 사람을 고용하고 있다.

employ.en: use a particular method, skill, or object for a purpose

ex.en: The team employed a new strategy to win the game.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 경기에서 이기기 위해 새로운 전략을 활용했다.

employee.en: a person who is paid to work for a company or organization

ex.en: All employees must wear an ID badge.
ex.ko: 모든 직원은 신분증을 착용해야 한다.

employer.en: a person or company that employs workers

ex.en: Her employer offered her a raise.
ex.ko: 그녀의 고용주는 그녀에게 임금 인상을 제안했다.

endeavor.en: a serious effort or attempt to do or achieve something

ex.en: The research project was a joint endeavor between two universities.
ex.ko: 그 연구 프로젝트는 두 대학의 공동 노력이었다.

ex.en: We will endeavor to finish the project on time.
ex.ko: 우리는 프로젝트를 제시간에 끝내려고 노력할 것이다.

endlessly.en: in a way that continues for a very long time or seems to have no end

ex.en: He talked endlessly about his new car.
ex.ko: 그는 새 차에 대해 끝없이 이야기했다.

endorse.en: publicly say that you support a plan, statement, or person

ex.en: The newspaper endorsed the opposition candidate.
ex.ko: 그 신문은 야당 후보를 지지했다.

endorse.en: say publicly that you support or approve of a product, especially in an advertisement

ex.en: The athlete was paid to endorse a sports drink.
ex.ko: 그 운동선수는 스포츠 음료를 홍보하는 대가로 돈을 받았다.

endorse.en: sign the back of a check to make it valid for cash or transfer

ex.en: You need to endorse the check before depositing it.
ex.ko: 수표를 입금하기 전에 배서를 해야 한다.

endurance.en: the ability to keep doing something difficult, painful, or tiring for a long time

ex.en: Running a marathon requires great endurance.
ex.ko: 마라톤을 뛰려면 대단한 지구력이 필요하다.

enduring.en: continuing to exist for a long time

ex.en: They formed an enduring friendship in college.
ex.ko: 그들은 대학에서 오래가는 우정을 쌓았다.

energy.cue: 일을 할 수 있게 하는 물리적인 힘
energy.en: the power that comes from things like electricity, heat, or motion, used to do work

ex.en: The car runs on electric energy.
ex.ko: 그 차는 전기 에너지로 움직인다.

energy.en: the physical or mental strength that allows you to do active things

ex.en: She always has so much energy in the morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아침마다 항상 활력이 넘친다.

energy.en: a source of power, such as electricity or fuel, used to run machines or heat buildings

ex.en: The country is investing in renewable energy.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 재생 에너지에 투자하고 있다.

enforce.en: make sure that a law or rule is obeyed

ex.en: The police enforce traffic laws in the city.
ex.ko: 경찰은 시내에서 교통 법규를 집행한다.

ex.en: They announced their engagement last weekend.
ex.ko: 그들은 지난 주말 약혼을 발표했다.

engagement.en: an arrangement to meet someone or do something at a particular time

ex.en: She had a prior engagement and couldn't attend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 선약이 있어서 참석할 수 없었다.

engagement.en: active involvement or interest in something

ex.en: The company wants to increase customer engagement online.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 온라인 고객 참여를 늘리고 싶어 한다.

engulf.en: completely cover or surround something, often suddenly and powerfully

ex.en: Flames quickly engulfed the entire building.
ex.ko: 화염이 순식간에 건물 전체를 뒤덮었다.

enhance.en: improve the quality, value, or attractiveness of something

ex.en: The new features enhance the app's performance.
ex.ko: 새 기능들이 그 앱의 성능을 향상시킨다.

enroll.en: officially put your name on a list to join a course, school, or program

ex.en: She enrolled in a Spanish class this semester.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이번 학기에 스페인어 수업에 등록했다.

ex.en: The government supports small enterprises with tax breaks.
ex.ko: 정부는 세제 혜택으로 소규모 기업들을 지원한다.

enterprise.en: a project or undertaking, especially one that is difficult or requires effort

ex.en: Building the bridge was a risky enterprise.
ex.ko: 그 다리를 건설하는 것은 위험 부담이 큰 사업이었다.

enterprise.en: the willingness and ability to start new projects or businesses

ex.en: The award recognizes enterprise and innovation in young business owners.
ex.ko: 그 상은 젊은 사업가들의 진취성과 혁신을 인정한다.

entertaining.en: enjoyable and interesting to watch, read, or listen to

ex.en: The movie was funny and entertaining.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 재미있고 즐거웠다.

enthusiastically.en: in a way that shows a lot of excitement and interest

ex.en: The crowd cheered enthusiastically for the team.
ex.ko: 관중들은 그 팀을 위해 열정적으로 환호했다.

entitle.en: give someone the right to have or do something

ex.en: This ticket entitles you to a free drink.
ex.ko: 이 티켓은 무료 음료를 받을 자격을 준다.

entitle.en: give a title to a book, film, or other work

ex.en: He wrote a novel entitled 'The Long Road Home'.
ex.ko: 그는 '먼 귀향길'이라는 제목의 소설을 썼다.

entrepreneur.en: a person who starts a business and takes on financial risk to do so

ex.en: She became a successful entrepreneur in her twenties.
ex.ko: 그녀는 20대에 성공한 기업가가 되었다.

environment.en: the natural world, including land, water, and air, in which people, animals, and plants live

ex.en: We should all try to protect the environment.
ex.ko: 우리 모두 환경을 보호하려고 노력해야 한다.

environment.en: the conditions or situation that surround a person or activity

ex.en: The company promotes a friendly work environment.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 우호적인 근무 환경을 지향한다.

environmentally.en: in a way that relates to the natural environment

ex.en: The factory was criticized for being environmentally harmful.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 환경적으로 해롭다는 비판을 받았다.

ex.en: More companies are switching to environmentally friendly packaging.
ex.ko: 점점 더 많은 회사들이 친환경 포장으로 바꾸고 있다.

equation.cue: 두 수·식이 같음을 나타내는 수학 표현
equation.en: a mathematical statement that shows two expressions are equal

ex.en: She solved the equation on the whiteboard.
ex.ko: 그녀는 화이트보드에서 그 방정식을 풀었다.

equation.en: a factor that must be considered when thinking about a situation

ex.en: Cost is an important part of the equation when choosing a car.
ex.ko: 차를 고를 때 비용은 중요하게 고려해야 할 요인이다.

equip.en: provide someone or something with the tools or equipment needed for a purpose

ex.en: The kitchen is equipped with modern appliances.
ex.ko: 그 주방은 현대적인 가전제품을 갖추고 있다.

equip.en: give someone the skills or knowledge they need to do something

ex.en: The course equips students with practical writing skills.
ex.ko: 그 강좌는 학생들에게 실용적인 글쓰기 능력을 갖추게 한다.

equity.en: the value of a company's shares, or the ownership share someone holds in it

ex.en: He invested in the company in exchange for equity.
ex.ko: 그는 지분을 받는 대가로 그 회사에 투자했다.

equity.en: the quality of being fair and treating everyone equally

ex.en: The policy aims to promote equity in education.
ex.ko: 그 정책은 교육에서의 형평성을 증진하는 것을 목표로 한다.

ex.en: The soldier stood erect during the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그 군인은 의식 동안 똑바로 서 있었다.

erect.en: build or put up a structure

ex.en: A new monument was erected in the town square.
ex.ko: 마을 광장에 새 기념비가 세워졌다.

erupt.en: (of a volcano) suddenly throw out lava, ash, and gas

ex.en: The volcano erupted after decades of silence.
ex.ko: 그 화산은 수십 년 만에 분출했다.

erupt.en: start suddenly and violently, especially violence, noise, or strong emotion

ex.en: Violence erupted after the referee's controversial decision.
ex.ko: 심판의 논란이 된 판정 이후 폭력 사태가 터졌다.

eruption.en: a sudden explosion of lava, ash, and gas from a volcano

ex.en: The eruption forced thousands of people to evacuate.
ex.ko: 그 화산 폭발로 수천 명이 대피해야 했다.

eruption.en: a sudden, violent outburst of emotion or activity

ex.en: The announcement caused an eruption of anger among workers.
ex.ko: 그 발표는 노동자들 사이에서 분노의 폭발을 일으켰다.

essayist.en: a writer known for writing essays

ex.en: She is a well-known essayist and critic.
ex.ko: 그녀는 잘 알려진 수필가이자 평론가다.

essentially.en: used to describe the basic or most important quality of something

ex.en: The two plans are essentially the same.
ex.ko: 그 두 계획은 본질적으로 같다.

estate.en: a large area of land, often with a big house, owned by one person or family

ex.en: The family owns a large estate in the countryside.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 시골에 넓은 사유지를 소유하고 있다.

estate.cue: 사람이 죽은 뒤 남기는 모든 재산
estate.en: all the money and property that a person owns, especially after they die

ex.en: His estate was divided among his three children.
ex.ko: 그의 재산은 세 자녀에게 나뉘었다.

estimated.en: calculated approximately, rather than known exactly

ex.en: The estimated cost of the project is one million dollars.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 추정 비용은 100만 달러다.

ex.en: She will be eternally grateful for their help.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그들의 도움에 영원히 감사할 것이다.

ethic.cue: 개인·집단이 지켜야 할 옳고 그름의 기준
ethic.en: a set of moral principles that guides a person's or group's behavior

ex.en: She has a strong work ethic.
ex.ko: 그녀는 투철한 직업 윤리를 가지고 있다.

ethnic.en: relating to a particular race, nation, or group of people who share a culture

ex.en: The city is known for its ethnic diversity.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 민족적 다양성으로 유명하다.

evacuate.en: move people away from a dangerous place to somewhere safer

ex.en: Residents were evacuated before the hurricane hit.
ex.ko: 허리케인이 오기 전에 주민들은 대피했다.

evacuation.en: the act of moving people away from a dangerous place to safety

ex.en: The evacuation of the coastal town began at dawn.
ex.ko: 그 해안 마을의 대피는 새벽에 시작되었다.

evaluate.en: judge or calculate the quality, importance, or value of something

ex.en: Teachers evaluate students' progress every semester.
ex.ko: 교사들은 매 학기 학생들의 진도를 평가한다.

evaluation.en: the process of judging the quality, importance, or value of something

ex.en: The manager conducts an annual performance evaluation.
ex.ko: 그 관리자는 매년 성과 평가를 실시한다.

everlasting.en: continuing forever or for a very long time

ex.en: They promised each other everlasting love.
ex.ko: 그들은 서로에게 영원한 사랑을 약속했다.

evidently.en: in a way that is clear and easy to see or understand from the available information

ex.en: Evidently, he had not read the instructions.
ex.ko: 명백히 그는 설명서를 읽지 않았다.

evil.en: morally wrong and harmful; extremely bad

ex.en: The story is about the battle between good and evil.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 선과 악의 싸움에 관한 것이다.

evil.en: wickedness or wrongdoing that causes great harm

ex.en: Poverty is one of the great evils of our society.
ex.ko: 빈곤은 우리 사회의 큰 폐해 중 하나다.

evolution.cue: 생물종이 여러 세대에 걸쳐 변화하는 과정
evolution.en: the gradual process by which living things change and develop over many generations

ex.en: Darwin's theory of evolution changed how we understand life on Earth.
ex.ko: 다윈의 진화론은 우리가 지구상의 생명체를 이해하는 방식을 바꾸었다.

evolution.en: the gradual development of something over time

ex.en: The evolution of smartphones has been remarkably fast.
ex.ko: 스마트폰의 발전 과정은 놀랍도록 빨랐다.

evolve.en: (of living things) develop gradually over many generations

ex.en: Birds are believed to have evolved from dinosaurs.
ex.ko: 새는 공룡에서 진화한 것으로 여겨진다.

evolve.en: develop gradually over a period of time

ex.en: Their relationship evolved from friendship into something deeper.
ex.ko: 그들의 관계는 우정에서 더 깊은 사이로 발전했다.

exactness.en: the quality of being completely correct or precise

ex.en: The scientist measured the sample with great exactness.
ex.ko: 그 과학자는 대단한 정확성으로 시료를 측정했다.

exaggerate.en: describe something as bigger, better, or worse than it really is

ex.en: He tends to exaggerate his achievements.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 업적을 과장하는 경향이 있다.

exaggeration.en: a statement that makes something seem bigger, better, or worse than it really is

ex.en: Saying it was the best day of my life is not an exaggeration.
ex.ko: 그날이 내 인생 최고의 날이었다는 건 과장이 아니다.

exalt.en: praise someone or something very highly

ex.en: The poem exalts the beauty of nature.
ex.ko: 그 시는 자연의 아름다움을 찬양한다.

exalt.en: raise someone to a higher rank or position

ex.en: He was exalted to the rank of general.
ex.ko: 그는 장군의 계급으로 승격되었다.

examinee.en: a person who is taking an exam or test

ex.en: All examinees must arrive thirty minutes early.
ex.ko: 모든 응시자는 30분 일찍 도착해야 한다.

exceed.en: be greater than a particular amount or limit

ex.en: The car's speed should not exceed the limit.
ex.ko: 차의 속도는 제한을 초과해서는 안 된다.

excel.en: be very good at doing something, especially better than most people

ex.en: She excels at mathematics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학에 뛰어나다.

exception.en: a person or thing that is not included in a general rule or pattern

ex.en: Most students passed, but there were a few exceptions.
ex.ko: 대부분의 학생은 합격했지만 몇 가지 예외가 있었다.

exception.en: (in the phrase 'take exception to') a feeling of being offended or disagreeing with something

ex.en: He took exception to her comments about his work.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 업무에 대한 그녀의 발언에 반감을 가졌다.

ex.en: She showed exceptional talent in music from a young age.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어릴 때부터 음악에 매우 뛰어난 재능을 보였다.

ex.en: The refund was granted only under exceptional circumstances.
ex.ko: 그 환불은 예외적인 상황에서만 허용되었다.

ex.en: It was an exceptionally cold winter this year.
ex.ko: 올해는 유난히 추운 겨울이었다.

excessive.en: more than what is necessary, normal, or acceptable

ex.en: The restaurant charges an excessive amount for delivery.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 배달비를 과도하게 청구한다.

exclaim.en: say something suddenly and loudly, especially because of surprise or strong emotion

ex.en: "Look out!" she exclaimed.
ex.ko: "조심해!" 그녀가 소리쳤다.

exclamation mark.en: the punctuation mark (!) used after a sentence that expresses strong feeling

ex.en: She ended the message with an exclamation mark.
ex.ko: 그녀는 메시지 끝에 느낌표를 붙였다.

exclude.en: deliberately leave someone or something out of a group, list, or consideration

ex.en: The price excludes tax and delivery.
ex.ko: 그 가격은 세금과 배송비를 제외한 것이다.

exclude.en: prevent someone from taking part in an activity or being part of a group

ex.en: She felt excluded from the group's conversations.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 무리의 대화에서 소외된다고 느꼈다.

exclusion.en: the act of not including someone or something, or of preventing them from taking part

ex.en: The report highlighted the social exclusion faced by minorities.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 소수자들이 겪는 사회적 배제를 조명했다.

exclusively.en: only, and involving no one or nothing else

ex.en: This offer is available exclusively to members.
ex.ko: 이 혜택은 회원에게만 독점적으로 제공된다.

excursion.en: a short trip taken for pleasure or a specific purpose

ex.en: The class went on an excursion to the museum.
ex.ko: 그 반은 박물관으로 소풍을 갔다.

execute.en: carry out or put into effect a plan or instruction

ex.en: The team executed the plan perfectly.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 계획을 완벽하게 실행했다.

execute.en: kill someone as a legal punishment

ex.en: The prisoner was executed at dawn.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 새벽에 처형되었다.

execute.en: (of a computer) run a program or instruction

ex.en: The computer executed the command in a few seconds.
ex.ko: 컴퓨터는 몇 초 만에 그 명령어를 실행했다.

execution.en: the act of doing or carrying out a plan, order, or piece of work

ex.en: The success of the project depends on careful execution.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트의 성공은 신중한 실행에 달려 있다.

execution.en: the act of killing someone as a legal punishment

ex.en: The execution was postponed at the last minute.
ex.ko: 그 처형은 막판에 연기되었다.

execution.en: (computing) the process of running a program or instruction

ex.en: The bug caused an error during program execution.
ex.ko: 그 버그는 프로그램 실행 중 오류를 일으켰다.

executive.en: a person who has a senior managerial role in a business

ex.en: She was promoted to senior executive last year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작년에 고위 임원으로 승진했다.

executive.en: relating to managing or making important decisions in a business or organization

ex.en: He made an executive decision to cancel the launch.
ex.ko: 그는 출시를 취소하는 경영상의 결정을 내렸다.

executive.cue: 법을 만드는 게 아니라 집행하는 정부 부문
executive.en: the branch of government responsible for putting laws into effect

ex.en: The executive and the legislature often disagree on policy.
ex.ko: 행정부와 입법부는 정책에 대해 자주 의견이 엇갈린다.

ex.en: The long hike exhausted the whole group.
ex.ko: 긴 하이킹은 그 무리 전체를 지치게 했다.

exhaust.en: use up all of a supply of something completely

ex.en: We've exhausted all our options.
ex.ko: 우리는 가능한 모든 방법을 다 써버렸다.

exhaust.en: the gases released from a vehicle's engine

ex.en: Exhaust fumes filled the underground parking lot.
ex.ko: 배기가스가 지하 주차장을 가득 채웠다.
